response: The internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense networking research, and ARPANET was first demonstrated in 1969 with a message sent from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. A major milestone came in 1983, when TCP/IP became the standard, creating the technical foundation of the modern internet; later, Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989, and Mosaic’s 1993 launch helped bring it to ordinary users. The text argues that the internet evolved from a small research network into a global public utility through key waves including the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, the rise of social media in the mid-2000s, smartphones after the iPhone’s 2007 launch, and cloud computing with AWS in 2006. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and supports nearly every part of modern life, including communication, commerce, and entertainment.
